growth_records.strain: NA1000 (wild type, per FDAA labeling methods)
growth_records.cite: Delaby et al. Phenotypic plasticity in cell elongation among closely related bacterial species. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60005-y. [PMC12130487]
growth_records.snippet: HADA was added to early exponential phase cells (OD600 ~0.25)... The cells were grown in PYE at 30 °C for 5% of their doubling time (5 min).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time derived from stated labeling pulse duration: text states the pulse was applied for '5% of their doubling time (5 min)', so doubling_time = 5 min / 0.05 = 100 min = 1.667 h. mu = ln2/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/1.667 = 0.416 h^-1. Medium composition (PYE recipe) not numerically detailed in this paper, only referenced to an external protocol. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.agit: 96 rpm (orbital, microplate); routine flask culture rpm not stated
growth_records.cite: Beroual et al. The noncoding RNA CcnA modulates the master cell cycle regulators CtrA and GcrA in Caulobacter crescentus. PLoS Biology (2022). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001528. [PMC8959179]
growth_records.snippet: A synchronized population of wild-type (WT) C. crescentus was used to collect cells at 15-min intervals in rich medium (generation time is 96 min).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (96 min = 1.6 h) explicitly reported in text for WT NA1000 synchronized in rich (PYE) medium; growth_rate_per_h = ln2/1.6 = 0.433 h-1. Temperature (30C) and aerobic batch culture taken from Materials and Methods statement that C. crescentus cultures were grown at 30C (as opposed to E. coli at 37C); not explicitly re-stated for this specific measurement, so medium confidence.

growth_records.strain: NA1000 Plac-ccnA-as (antisense ccnA overexpression, pSRK vector, IPTG-inducible)
growth_records.agit: 96 rpm (orbital, microplate); routine flask culture rpm not stated
growth_records.cite: Beroual et al. The noncoding RNA CcnA modulates the master cell cycle regulators CtrA and GcrA in Caulobacter crescentus. PLoS Biology (2022). DOI:10.1371/journal.pbio.3001528. [PMC8959179]
growth_records.snippet: Cell growth was monitored overnight with a Spark-TM at 30°C... Doubling time of Plac-ccnA-as cells equals to 201.96 min +/- 11.66 with 1 mM IPTG.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (201.96 min = 3.366 h) explicitly reported for the ccnA-antisense overexpression strain grown in PYE + 1 mM IPTG at 30C (96-well plate, Spark plate reader); growth_rate_per_h = ln2/3.366 = 0.206 h-1. This is a genetically engineered strain (ccnA antisense overexpression), not wild-type baseline metabolism, included as a distinct reported growth observation.

growth_records.medium: M2 minimal medium + xylan (0.05% w/v, sole carbon source)
growth_records.cite: D’Souza et al. Nutrient complexity triggers transitions between solitary and colonial growth in bacterial populations. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-00953-7. [PMC8397785]
growth_records.snippet: The number of cells in a growth chamber doubled on average every 3.6 ± 0.54 h in xylan (mean ± 95% CI...) but took 15.50 ± 7.55 h to double in xylose
growth_records.notes: Paper reports population-doubling time of 3.6 ± 0.54 h (mean ± 95% CI, n=9 chambers) for C. crescentus CB15 microcolonies growing on 0.05% xylan in M2 minimal medium within microfluidic chambers (30C). mu = ln2/3.6 h = 0.1925 h^-1 (calculated by curator). Individual-chamber range for the same condition, given in Fig.2 legend, was doubling time = 2.89-4.15 h (exponential model, R2=0.92-0.99), i.e. mu range 0.167-0.240 h^-1.

growth_records.medium: M2 minimal medium + xylose (0.05% w/v, sole carbon source)
growth_records.cite: D’Souza et al. Nutrient complexity triggers transitions between solitary and colonial growth in bacterial populations. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-00953-7. [PMC8397785]
growth_records.snippet: ...but took 15.50 ± 7.55 h to double in xylose (mean ± 95% CI, Fig. 2c)
growth_records.notes: Paper reports population-doubling time of 15.50 ± 7.55 h (mean ± 95% CI, n=9 chambers) for C. crescentus CB15 on 0.05% xylose in M2 minimal medium within microfluidic chambers (30C). mu = ln2/15.50 h = 0.0447 h^-1 (calculated by curator). This is the dispersing/solitary-growth condition (cells do not aggregate on xylose); note this doubling time reflects sessile-cell accumulation in the chamber, which is lower than single-cell growth rate because swarmer daughter cells disperse away rather than because of slow division per se (paper notes similar per-cell division propensity in both substrates).

growth_records.cite: Hartl et al. Untargeted metabolomics links glutathione to bacterial cell cycle progression. Nature Metabolism (2020). DOI:10.1038/s42255-019-0166-0. [PMC7035108]
growth_records.snippet: These synchronized cultures and asynchronous control cultures (n=3 biological replicates each) were then followed throughout one cell cycle, as judged by one doubling in biomass (doubling time 159 ± 5 and 147 ± 6 min respectively)
growth_records.notes: Synchronized swarmer-cell culture. Reported doubling time td=159 min = 159/60 = 2.65 h. mu=ln2/td = 0.6931/2.65 = 0.2615 /h. Grown in M2 minimal (Ely et al., MgCl2 replaced by MgSO4) + 0.2% glucose (M2G), 30 C, 150 rpm shake flask. No substrate uptake/secretion fluxes measured (the 10 mM glucose/gCDW value in the paper is an assumed FBA model constraint, in silico, not a measurement — excluded). Full M2 salt composition not enumerated in-text (cited to Ely et al.), so medium_composition_stated=false.

growth_records.cite: Hartl et al. Untargeted metabolomics links glutathione to bacterial cell cycle progression. Nature Metabolism (2020). DOI:10.1038/s42255-019-0166-0. [PMC7035108]
growth_records.snippet: These synchronized cultures and asynchronous control cultures (n=3 biological replicates each) were then followed throughout one cell cycle, as judged by one doubling in biomass (doubling time 159 ± 5 and 147 ± 6 min respectively)
growth_records.notes: Asynchronous (control) culture, second value in 'respectively'. Reported doubling time td=147 min = 147/60 = 2.45 h. mu=ln2/td = 0.6931/2.45 = 0.2829 /h. Same M2G medium (M2 minimal, MgCl2->MgSO4, +0.2% glucose), 30 C, 150 rpm shake flask. No uptake/secretion fluxes measured. Full M2 salt composition cited to Ely et al., not enumerated in-text.
